entry.title: Chap 5
entry.content:
• Assumption 5.1 [Ergodic Stationarity]: \(\left\{ Y_t, X_t'\right\}_{t=1}^{'n}\) is jointly stationary and ergodic
  • Assumption 5.2 [Linearity]: \(Y_t = X_t' \beta^o +\varepsilon_t\)
  • Assumption 5.3 [Correct Model Specification]: \(E(\varepsilon_t|X_t)=0\) with \(E(\varepsilon_t^2)=\sigma^2<\infty\)
  • Assumption 5.4 [Nonsingularity]: \(Q=E(X_tX_t')\) is symmetric, finite andnonsingular
  • Assumption 5.5 [MDS]: \(\left\{X_t\varepsilon_t\right\}\) is MDSwith respect to the \(\sigma\)-fieldgenerated by \(\left\{X_t\varepsilon_t,s<t\right\}\) and \(\mathop{V}\limits_{K\times K}\equivvar(X_t\varepsilon_t)=E(X_tX_t'\varepsilon_t^2)\) is finiteand PD.

5.3 Consistency

For proof, we introduce WLLN for Ergodic StationaryProcess

  • If \(\left\{ Z_t \right\}\) isa stationary ergodic process with \(E(Z_t)=\mu\) and \(E|Z_t|<\infty\). Then \(\bar Z_n \xrightarrow{p} \mu\)

Under Assumption 5.1-5.4, \[\hat \beta - \beta^o \xrightarrow{p} 0\]

5.4 Asymptotic Normality

For proof, we introduce CLT for Ergodic StationaryMDS

  • If \(\left\{ Z_t \right\}\) isstationary ergodic MDS with \(var(Z_t)=V\) finite, symmetric and positive definite. Then as\(\sqrt{n} \bar Z_n \xrightarrow{d}N(0,V)\)
HTK

Under Assumption 5.1-5.5, \[\sqrt{n} (\hat \beta - \beta^o ) \xrightarrow{d} N(0,Q^{-1}VQ^{-1})\]

HMK

Under Assumption 5.1-5.5 and conditional homoskedasticity \(E(\varepsilon_t^2|X_t)=\sigma^2\)(Assumption 5.6) \[\sqrt{n} (\hat \beta - \beta^o ) \xrightarrow{d} N(0,\sigma^2Q^{-1})\]

5.5 Asymptotic VarianceEstimator

HMK

Under Assumption 5.1-5.5, \[s^2 \xrightarrow{p}\sigma^2\] and thus \[s^2\hat Q^{-1} \xrightarrow{p} \sigma^2Q^{-1}\]

HTK

Under Assumption 5.1-5.5 and \(E(X_{jt}^4)<\infty\) for \(0\leq j \leq k\) and \(E(\varepsilon_t^4)<\infty\) (Assumption5.7), \[\hat V \equiv \frac{1}{n}\sum_{t=1}^nX_tX_t'e_t^2 \xrightarrow{p} V\] and thus \[\hat Q^{-1} \hat V\hat Q^{-1} \xrightarrow{p}Q^{-1}VQ^{-1}\]

5.6 Hypothesis Testing

HMK

Under Assumption 5.1-5.5 and conditional homoskedasticity \(E(\varepsilon_t^2|X_t)=\sigma^2\)(Assumption 5.6), under \(\textbf{H}_0\)

$$ \[\begin{align}\text{when }J=1: \quad & T = \frac{R \hat \beta - r}{\sqrt{s^2} R(X'X)^{-1} R'} \xrightarrow{p} N(0,1) \\\text{when }J\geq1: \quad & W = J \cdot F = \frac{(R\hat \beta -r)'[R(X'X)^{-1}R']^{-1}(R\hat \beta - r)}{s^2}\xrightarrow{d} \chi_{J}^2\end{align}\] $$

HTK

Under Assumption 5.1-5.5 and 5.7, under \(\textbf{H}_0\)

$$ \[\begin{align}\text{when } J=1: \quad &T_r = \frac{\sqrt{n}(R\hat \beta -r )}{\sqrt{R \hat Q^{-1} \hat V \hatQ^{-1} R'}} \xrightarrow{d} N(0,1) \\\text{when } J\geq 1: \quad &W_r = n (R\hat \beta -r )' [R \hat Q^{-1} \hat V \hat Q^{-1}R']^{-1}(R\hat \beta -r ) \xrightarrow{d} \chi_{J}^2\end{align}\] $$

Chap 6 HAC Estimation

  • Assumption 6.1 [Ergodic stationarity]: \(\left\{ Y_t, X_t'\right\}_{t=1}^{'n}\) is an observable stationary ergodicprocess.
  • Assumption 6.2 [Linearity]: \(Y_t = X_t' \beta^o +\varepsilon_t\)
  • Assumption 6.3 [Correct model specification]: \(E(\varepsilon_t|X_t)=0\)
  • Assumption 6.4 [Nonsingularity]: \(\mathop{Q}\limits_{K\timesK}=E(X_tX_t')\) is symmetric, finite and nonsingular.
  • Assumption 6.5 [Long-run variance]:
      1. \(\sum_{j=-\infty}^{\infty} ||\Gamma(j)||< ∞\) where $ (j) cov(X_tt, X{t-j}_{t-j})$ and \(V = \sum_{j=-\infty}^{\infty} \Gamma(j)\)is PD
      1. $E(X_tt|X{t-j}{t-j},X{t-j-1}_{t-j-1},) 0 j $
      1. \(\sum_{j=0}^\infty[E(r_j'r_j)]^{1/2}<\infty\)Where \(r_j=E(X_t\varepsilon_t|X_{t-j}\varepsilon_{t-j},X_{t-j-1}\varepsilon_{t-j-1},\cdots)-E(X_t\varepsilon_t|X_{t-j-1}\varepsilon_{t-j-1},X_{t-j-2}\varepsilon_{t-j-2},\cdots)\)

Consistency

Under Assumption 6.1-6.5.a \[\hat \beta \xrightarrow{p} \beta^o\]

Asymptotic Normality

For proof, we introduce CLT for Zero Mean Ergodic StationaryProcesses

  • Suppose \(\left\{Z_t\right\}\)is a stationary ergodic process with
    • \(E(Z_t)=0\);
    • \(V = \sum_{j=-\infty}^{\infty}\Gamma(j)\) is finite and nonsingular, where \(\Gamma(j)=E(Z_tZ_{t-j}')\);
    • \(E(Z_t|Z_{t-j},Z_{t-j-1},\cdots)\xrightarrow{q.m.}0\);
    • \(\sum_{j=0}^\infty[E(r_j'r_j)]^{1/2}<\infty\)where \(r_j=E(Z_t|Z_{t-j},Z_{t-j-1},\cdots)-E(X_t\varepsilon_t|X_{t-j-1}\varepsilon_{t-j-1},X_{t-j-2}\varepsilon_{t-j-2},\cdots)\)

​ Then, \(\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}} \bar Z_n\xrightarrow{d} N(0,V)\)

Under Assumption 6.1-6.5 \[\sqrt{n}(\hat \beta -\beta^o) \xrightarrow{d} N(0, Q^{-1}VQ^{-1}) \text{where } V = \sum_{j=-\infty}^{\infty} \Gamma(j)\]

Asymptotic VarianceEstimator

To estimate the asymptotic variance \(V =\sum_{j=-\infty}^{\infty} \Gamma(j)\), first consider a naiveestimator \[\hat V = \sum_{j=-(n-1)}^{n-1}\hat\Gamma(j)\] where \(\hat\Gamma(j)\xrightarrow{p}\Gamma(j)\), but \(\hatV \nrightarrow^p V\) since there are too many terms and variancemay explosive.

Then, we consider the truncated sum \[\hat V = \sum_{j=-p}^{p}\hat\Gamma(j)\] But when \(p\) is fixed, wecould expect \(\hat V\xrightarrow{p}\sum_{j=-p}^{p}\Gamma(j) \neq V\).

Hence, we should let \(p\)grows to infinity as \(n \rightarrow\infty\) but cannot grow as fast as sample size \(n\). Otherwise, the variance of\(\hat V\) will never vanish to zero.Therefore, to ensure consistency of \(\hatV\) to \(V\), we should balancethe bias and the vaiance of \(\hat V\)properly. Consider \[\hat V = \sum_{j=-p_n}^{p_n}\hat\Gamma(j), \text{ where } p_n\rightarrow \infty, p_n/n \rightarrow 0\] Although this estimator is consistent for \(V\), it may not be positive semi-definitefor all \(n\). So, use a weightedaverage estimator where weighting function \(k(j/p_n)\) is kernel function. \[\hat V = \sum_{j=-p_n}^{p_n}k(j/p_n)\hat\Gamma(j)\]

Hypothethis Test

Under Assuption 6.1-6.5 and directly assume \(\hat V \xrightarrow{p}V\) (Assumption 6.6),then under \(\textbf{H}_0\)

entry.title: Hello World
entry.content:
Welcome to Hexo! This is your veryfirst post. Check documentation formore info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find theanswer in troubleshooting oryou can ask me on GitHub.

Quick Start

Create a new post

More info: Writing

Run server

More info: Server

Generate static files

More info: Generating

Deploy to remote sites

More info: Deployment
